hypotheses.description: Long integrated oil majors and independent producers as Strait of Hormuz transit risk drives crude prices higher
hypotheses.causal_chain: Iran escalation risk → Hormuz chokepoint supply disruption fears → WTI/Brent crude spike → oil producer cash flows and stock prices rise
hypotheses.trigger_description: When WTI crude breaks above $85/bbl on geopolitical headlines and implied Hormuz disruption risk premium exceeds 5%

hypotheses.description: Short airlines and shipping companies facing margin compression from elevated energy costs without pricing power
hypotheses.causal_chain: Iran conflict escalation → oil price spike to $85+ → jet fuel and bunker fuel costs rise 15-25% → airlines/shippers cannot immediately pass through costs → margin compression over 5-10 days
hypotheses.trigger_description: When WTI crude exceeds $85/bbl and stays elevated for 3+ consecutive days, with airline fuel hedges already locked in at lower prices

hypotheses.description: Long defensive/safe-haven assets (long-duration Treasuries, gold) as geopolitical uncertainty spikes risk-off demand
hypotheses.causal_chain: Iran escalation narrative accelerates → equity volatility and tail-risk fears rise → capital rotates from equities to duration and gold → TLT and GLD outperform SPY
hypotheses.trigger_description: When VIX exceeds 20 and stays elevated, and 10Y Treasury yield drops below 4.0% on flight-to-safety flows

hypotheses.description: Aggressive Fed rate hold/hawkishness pressures refiners and airlines that cannot pass through higher financing costs and fuel expenses to consumers in near term
hypotheses.causal_chain: Central bank maintains restrictive rates → refiners (USO/XLE already priced in) face higher capex/debt servicing costs + airlines face higher jet fuel costs → margin compression in both sectors over 3-10 days as Q earnings guidance deteriorates
hypotheses.trigger_description: When Fed speakers reinforce 'higher for longer' stance or next CPI print remains sticky above 3.5% YoY
source_headlines: Rates Spark: Aggressive Central Bank Discount Remains Sticky

hypotheses.description: If Fed tightness persists and inflation remains sticky, bond yields (IEF, TLT already moving) become attractive relative to equities, driving rotation into fixed income and away from growth equities
hypotheses.causal_chain: Sticky inflation + hawkish Fed → real yields rise and remain elevated → QQQ (growth-heavy, rate-sensitive) underperforms relative to bond ETFs (IEF, TLT) → capital rotates into duration over 5-10 days
hypotheses.trigger_description: When 10Y yield breaks above 4.3% and stays there, or when Fed speakers explicitly reject near-term cuts
source_headlines: Rates Spark: Aggressive Central Bank Discount Remains Sticky

hypotheses.description: Inflation persistence narrative (diesel prices, Fed hawkishness) accelerates demand for inflation hedges; gold miners and commodity producers not yet fully re-rated despite 3-day article surge
hypotheses.causal_chain: Media mentions of sticky inflation + diesel/energy spikes → investors increase allocation to inflation hedges (gold, commodities) → upstream beneficiaries (gold miners, oil producers) see sustained bid over 5-10 days
hypotheses.trigger_description: When gold breaks above $2,050/oz or WTI crude sustains above $85/bbl for 2+ consecutive days
